QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_21

question.text: Trailing Twelve Months (TTM) earnings refer to:

answer.text: Earnings projected for the next 12 months
Earnings for the most recent 12-month period ending in the current month
Earnings reported in the latest annual report only
Average earnings over the last 5 years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TTM earnings are the earnings of the most recent 12-month period, calculated on a rolling basis ending in the current month. **The Trap:** TTM is backward-looking (most recent 12 months), not forward-looking. It differs from the latest annual report which may be dated. **Cross-Exam Context:** TTM concept is used in PE ratio calculations across NISM Series XV and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** TTM gives a more current picture than annual earnings, especially when there are seasonal variations or recent growth changes.
